config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the number. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the number in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the number. See [https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/number/#device-class](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/number/#device-class) for a list of available options.

config_entries.label: Unit Of Measurement
config_entries.description: Manually set the unit of measurement for the number.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Entity Category
config_entries.description: The category of the entity. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/#generic-properties) for a list of available options. Set to `""` to remove the default entity category.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Defines how the number should be displayed in the frontend. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/number/#properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/number/#properties) for a list of available options. Defaults to `"auto"`.

config_entries.label: Internal
config_entries.description: Mark this component as internal. Internal components will not be exposed to the frontend (like Home Assistant). Only specifying an `id` without a `name` will implicitly set this to true.

config_entries.label: Mode
config_entries.description: Defines how the number should be displayed in the frontend. See [https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/number/#properties](https://developers.home-assistant.io/docs/core/entity/number/#properties) for a list of available options. Defaults to `"auto"`.

config_entries.label: Gate Move Sensitivity
config_entries.description: Gate move threshold calibration sensitivity factor. Defaults to 0.5 with a range of 0-1, 0 = high and 1 = low. May contain any options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number#config-number).

config_entries.label: Name
config_entries.description: The name for the number. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Icon
config_entries.description: Manually set the icon to use for the number in the frontend.

config_entries.label: Device Class
config_entries.description: The device class for the number. See [https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/number/#device-class](https://www.home-assistant.io/integrations/number/#device-class) for a list of available options.

config_entries.label: Unit Of Measurement
config_entries.description: Manually set the unit of measurement for the number.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID for code generation. At least one of **id** and **name** must be specified.

config_entries.label: Disabled By Default
config_entries.description: If true, then this entity should not be added to any client's frontend, (usually Home Assistant) without the user manually enabling it (via the Home Assistant UI). Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Gate Select
config_entries.description:
**Set together with:** `move_threshold`, `still_threshold` (all-or-none).

Selection of gate value to edit (gate # from 0 to 15). Gate thresholds are edited by selecting the gate number and then adjusting the move and still thresholds. **Note that when this variable is defined, the component will run in compact UI mode and the following two configuration variables are then required:** May contain any options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number#config-number).

config_entries.label: Max Gate Distance
config_entries.description:
**Set together with:** `min_gate_distance`, `presence_timeout` (all-or-none).

Maximum gate for movement detection. Value from 1 to 15. Each gate detects movement and still energy at 70 cm increments. At the maximum value, the sensor's range equates to approximately 12 meters; however, accuracy at this range may be diminished. The recommended maximum value is 12 which results in approximately 9 meters. **Required** when `presence_timeout` and `min_gate_distance` are specified. May contain any options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number#config-number).

config_entries.label: Min Gate Distance
config_entries.description:
**Set together with:** `max_gate_distance`, `presence_timeout` (all-or-none).

Minimum distance for move or still energy detection. Value between 0 and `max_gate_distance` - 1. Each increment equals 70 cm. Defaults to 1. **Required** when `presence_timeout` and `max_gate_distance` is specified. May contain any options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number#config-number).

config_entries.label: Presence Timeout
config_entries.description:
**Set together with:** `max_gate_distance`, `min_gate_distance` (all-or-none).

The time in seconds during which the occupied state (presence) will persist after presence is no longer detected. Any energy detection within the time window restarts the countdown from this value. Defaults to 120s. **Required** when `min_gate_distance` and `max_gate_distance` is specified. May contain any options from [Number](https://esphome.io/components/number#config-number).
